title: Send Dash Engine Alerts to Google Glass
desc: Attention all Google Glass Owners,With this recipe, you can now get real-time engine alerts delivered directly to your Google Glass as soon as the light comes onNo searching for engine codes as you will actually see the real reasonFrom Loose Gas Caps to a bad fuel pump, know exactly what is wrong with your car.In order to use this application, you need the following1) Download the "Dash" application for Android from the Google Play Store2) Purchase a Bluetooth OBDC2 Dongle (Around $15.00 on Amazon) ( http://www.amazon.com/gp/search/?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=390957&index=aps&keywords=bluetooth%20OBD&linkCode=ur2&tag=kmyers-20&linkId=VHWFXXXU5YW7HNRV )3) Pair your Android Phone to your OBDC2 Dongle following the instructions in Dash.by.4) Go through the Dash setup wizard5) Activate the IFTTT Glass Channel6) Activate the IFTTT Dash Channel7) Activate this recipeThis should work for any car with an OBDC2 (On Board Diagnostic 2) connection portAny car made after 1997 contains this portFeel free to follow me on Google Plus (http://plus.kmyers.me) if you have any issues or questions.

title: Ganga A Divinity in Flow
desc: Ganga or the Ganges is no ordinary riverInstead she represents the essence of a civilization, form of culture and substance of a faithShe is in fact, a way of lifeShe is a living goddess whose importance in the day to day life of a believer cannot be described in words or through picturesThis book is only a small but sincere effort to present the Mother Ganga, before the reader, in all her varied dimensionsThis book is an ode sung in praise of Ganga which has been variedly described as waters of creation, symbol of life, river of India and source of all knowledgeThis is also a pictorial depiction of divinity-in-flow and personification-of-purity in the form of a river which is stated to have descended from heaven for the emancipation of mankindThe uniqueness of the book is in its approach to the subjectIt is not a focus on the popular destinations of Rishikesh, Haridwar and VaranasiThree years of extensive travel along the 2,510 km long river ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ right from the Gangotri glacier (the source of the river), down to the Ganga Sagar, where it merges into the Bay of Bengal, this is a visual delightOver 400 pictures portray the daily life of millions of people, as the vivacious river flows through their hearts, day after day, sustaining and urifying on esoteric and mundane levelsThe inimitable endeavour is reflected in the array of places, people and life around the Divine River, as it grows from gentle streams, flows through the eponymous planes of northern India and is consumed through the vast delta in the Sundarbans.
